venue: Westmoreland Country Club, 7100 Mellon Rd, Export, PA 15632

ads.title: Brand introduction image
ads.primary_text:
Confluence Financial Partners is hosting a private event at Westmoreland Country Club.

Tuesday, May 5 at 6:00 PM.

Four leaders. One evening:

Greg Weimer — Chief Executive Officer
Gregory Weimer, CFP®, CPA — President
Bill Winkeler, CFA, CFP® — Chief Investment Officer
Chuck Zuzak, CFA, CFP® — Director of Wealth Planning

An evening of clarity focused on your financial future.

→ Economic trends and portfolio considerations
→ Forward-looking planning considerations for your future
→ Family meetings, estate planning and thoughtful giving
→ Integration of technology in financial planning

Complimentary cocktails and hors d'oeuvres. Seating is limited.
ads.headline: An Evening of Clarity
ads.hook: Confluence Financial Partners is hosting a private event for Pittsburgh families.

stories.image: Panelist credential stack (4 headshots vertical with names/titles) on deep green (#12493C) background
stories.secondary_text: Complimentary | Tue, May 5 | Westmoreland Country Club

ads.arc: Institutional credibility — C-suite panel + firm brand (authority)
ads.image_direction:
**Format:** 1080x1080 (square) + 1080x1920 (vertical) | **Placement:** Feed, Stories, Reels
**Layout:** Headshot grid over event photo (Archetype 1 + 3 hybrid)

```
COMPOSITION:
+--------------------------------------+
|  LOGO (top-center, small, white)     |
|  [Steelers badge, small, right       |
|   of logo — nav accent]             |
|                                      |
|  [BACKGROUND: Previous panel or      |
|   event photo — the team presenting, |
|   speaking at a podium, or seated    |
|   at a discussion table. Dimmed to   |
|   ~30% opacity over navy base so     |
|   headshots read clearly on top.]    |
|                                      |
|  "Meet Your Panel"                   |
|  (Ivypresto, white, centered)        |
|                                      |
|  [Greg Weimer]   [Gregory Weimer]    |
|   CEO             CFP®, CPA         |
|                   President          |
|                                      |
|  [Bill Winkeler]  [Chuck Zuzak]      |
|   CFA, CFP®       CFA, CFP®         |
|   Chief Investment Dir. of Wealth    |
|   Officer         Planning           |
|                                      |
|  (Circular headshots ~200px dia,     |
|   white border 3px, 2x2 grid)       |
|  (Names: Rival Sans, white, 16pt)    |
|  (Credentials: Rival Sans, gold,     |
|   13pt) (Titles: white 70%, 12pt)    |
|                                      |
|  Tue, May 5 | 6 PM                   |
|  Westmoreland Country Club           |
|  (Rival Sans, small, white 80%)      |
|                                      |
|  [RESERVE YOUR SEAT] (outlined)      |
+--------------------------------------+
```

**Headshot layout:** 2x2 grid centered. Match the flyer's circular crop style (see page 2 of event flyer for reference — same headshot treatment, same credential format). Each circle ~200px diameter with 3px white border. Names centered below each circle, credentials in gold, titles in dimmed white.

**Background photo:** A previous Confluence panel event, speaking engagement, or team presentation photo. Dim to ~30% opacity over navy #0b1a2e base so the headshots pop. The background gives context — these people have done this before — without competing with the headshot grid. Check `assets/photos/branded/Highlights/` for event or presentation shots. If no previous panel photo exists, use a meeting/conference room shot with the team.

**Text overlay:** ~25% coverage. "Meet Your Panel" headline above the grid, event details and CTA below.

**Reference:** Event flyer page 2 "Meet the Panel" section — same headshots, same circular crop, same credential format. The ad version layers this over a dimmed event photo and adds the 2x2 stacked layout instead of the flyer's single row.

ads.title: Educational carousel (5 cards)
ads.format: Carousel, 5 cards at 1080x1080 (Feed) + static image 1080x1920 (Stories/Reels fallback)
ads.primary_text:
Four leaders. Relevant topics. One evening at Westmoreland Country Club. You're invited.

Tuesday, May 5 at 6:00 PM. Swipe to see what the panel covers.

Complimentary cocktails and hors d'oeuvres. Seating is limited.
ads.headline: What Our Leadership Panel Covers
ads.hook: Four leaders. Relevant topics. One evening at Westmoreland Country Club. You're invited.

cards.label: Hook
cards.caption:
Your financial plan touches investments, estate documents, taxes, family goals.

Often, these areas aren't reviewed together. That's what this evening is about.

cards.label: Economy & Portfolio
cards.caption:
What does the current economy mean for your portfolio?

Our CIO, Bill Winkeler, CFA, CFP®, will walk through what he's watching in the markets and what it could mean for the years ahead.

cards.label: Estate & Family Meetings
cards.caption:
When was the last time your family sat down to talk about the plan?

Estate documents get drafted and filed. Life changes. We'll discuss elements of a productive family meeting.

cards.label: Charitable Giving & Pitfalls
cards.caption:
Charitable giving is one of the most misunderstood parts of a financial plan.

We'll also cover the common pitfalls we see families make.

cards.label: CTA
cards.caption:
Tuesday, May 5 at 6:00 PM
Westmoreland Country Club, Export, PA

Complimentary cocktails and hors d'oeuvres. Seating is limited. Reserve yours.

ads.arc: Content preview — relevant topics from four leaders (reciprocity)

**Card 2 label:** Forward-Looking Planning
**Card 3 label:** Estate & Family Meetings
**Card 4 label:** Economy & Portfolio

**Visual continuity:** All 5 cards share the same navy background, same font sizes, same logo position. Cards 2-4 use the diagonal line texture as subtle brand element. Gold accent color ties the number/label across content cards.

ads.title: Peace of mind image
ads.primary_text:
The next few years can be especially important. Your portfolio, estate plan, and tax strategy all interact with each other. Often, they're managed separately.

Tuesday, May 5 at Westmoreland Country Club.

Our CEO, President, CIO, and Director of Wealth Planning will walk through it together — economic trends, estate planning, giving strategies, and decisions that can have a meaningful impact over time.

Complimentary cocktails and hors d'oeuvres. Seating is limited.
ads.headline: Are the Pieces Working Together?
ads.hook: The next few years can be especially important.

stories.image: Emotional "pieces of your financial life" visual — portfolio, estate, tax as connected elements
stories.secondary_text: Relevant topics | Tue, May 5 | Westmoreland Country Club

ads.arc: Emotional weight of peak earning years — decisions that can have a meaningful impact over time (loss aversion)
ads.image_direction:
**Format:** 1080x1080 (square) + 1080x1920 (vertical) | **Placement:** Feed, Stories, Reels
**Layout:** Photo-dominant (Archetype 1) — matches inspiration Ads 01/02 most closely

```
COMPOSITION:
+--------------------------------------+
|  LOGO (top-center, navy)             |
|                                      |
|  [Lifestyle photo: couple (50s-60s)  |
|   in Pittsburgh-area setting —       |
|   autumn park, suburban garden,      |
|   or country club grounds.           |
|   Relaxed posture, warm tones,       |
|   open sky or foliage above.]        |
|                                      |
|  "Confidence in                      |
|   What You've Built."                |
|  (Ivypresto, dark navy, centered     |
|   — positioned in open sky area)     |
|                                      |
|  [RESERVE YOUR SEAT] (outlined)      |
|                                      |
+--------------------------------------+
```

**Photo direction:** Lifestyle couple in warm outdoor Pittsburgh-area setting. Key: natural light, relaxed body language, autumn foliage or green park landscape visible. Text overlays onto open sky area (no gradient needed if sky provides contrast). Logo in navy (not white) since background is light. Use `assets/photos/stock-website/ConfluenceWeb Images/` for couple lifestyle photos — select images that feel Pittsburgh-appropriate (no beach, no palm trees, no waterfront).

**This is the emotional anchor ad.** The photo should feel aspirational but real — "this is your life, we help you protect it." The setting should feel like Western Pennsylvania, not a resort.

ads.hook: Confluence has been in Pittsburgh as long as the families we work with.

stories.image: Pittsburgh skyline or landmark photo with Confluence branding

ads.arc: Pittsburgh community identity — local imagery + community language (in-group bias)
ads.image_direction:
**Format:** 1080x1920 (vertical) | **Placement:** Stories, Reels
**Layout:** Photo-dominant with text overlays — Stories/Reels format

```
STORIES FRAME:
+--------------------+
|                    |
| [Pittsburgh        |
|  landmark photo:   |
|  skyline from      |
|  Mt. Washington,   |
|  Point State Park, |
|  bridges at        |
|  golden hour,      |
|  or fall foliage   |
|  along the rivers  |
|  — full-bleed      |
|  background]       |
|                    |
| "You didn't build  |
|  this life by      |
|  accident."        |
| (Ivypresto, white, |
|  text-shadow,      |
|  centered)         |
|                    |
| [Steelers badge,   |
|  small, centered]  |
|                    |
| Complimentary      |
| cocktails + panel  |
| Tue, May 5         |
| (Rival Sans,       |
|  white 80%)        |
|                    |
| [RESERVE YOUR SEAT]|
| (pill CTA sticker, |
|  white bg)         |
+--------------------+
```

**Photo direction:** Pittsburgh landmark photo — the city IS the subject. Pittsburgh skyline at golden hour from Mt. Washington, Point State Park fountain with bridges in background, or the confluence of the three rivers with fall foliage. One strong image, not a montage. The photo should make a Pittsburgh resident feel pride — "this is my city, this is my life."

**Steelers badge:** Small, centered below the headline — reinforces the local connection without dominating. The Steelers badge is the accent that says "this firm is part of this city."

**Key visual difference from other ads:** No faces, no advisor — this is about PLACE and IDENTITY. The viewer sees their own city reflected.

ads.title: Infographic / visual concept
ads.primary_text:
Relevant topics. Four leaders. One evening.

Tuesday, May 5 at Westmoreland Country Club.

Our leadership team will cover a range of topics:

→ Economic trends and portfolio considerations
→ Forward-looking planning considerations for your future
→ Family meetings, estate planning and thoughtful giving
→ Integration of technology in financial planning

Complimentary cocktails and hors d'oeuvres. Seating is limited.
ads.headline: Relevant Topics. One Evening.
ads.hook: Four leaders. Relevant topics. One evening.

stories.image: Vertical six-tile infographic — stacked topic tiles with icons, panelist headshots at bottom
stories.secondary_text: Tue, May 5 | 6 PM | Westmoreland Country Club

ads.infographic_direction:
Six-panel visual — one tile per topic, each with an icon or simple illustration:
- Forward-looking planning
- Estate planning & family wealth decisions
- Intentional giving strategies
- Lessons families wish they'd learned sooner
- Economic trends & portfolio management
- Technology & innovation in financial planning

Panelist headshots along the bottom. Event details (date, venue, complimentary) as footer bar. Dark deep green (#12493C) background, mint (#7EFBC8) accents, Confluence branding.
ads.arc: Six-topic panel overview infographic (breadth of expertise)
ads.image_direction:
**Format:** 1080x1080 (square) + 1080x1920 (vertical) | **Placement:** Feed, Stories, Reels
**Layout:** Six-tile topic overview (custom)

```
COMPOSITION (1:1 SQUARE):
+--------------------------------------+
|  LOGO (top-center, white)            |
|                                      |
|  "Six Topics. One Evening."          |
|  (Ivypresto, white, centered)        |
|                                      |
+------------------+-------------------+
|  [Icon]          |  [Icon]           |
|  Forward-looking |  Family meetings  |
|  planning        |  & estate         |
|                  |  planning         |
+------------------+-------------------+
|  [Icon]          |  [Icon]           |
|  Charitable      |  Lessons learned  |
|  giving          |  & common         |
|                  |  pitfalls         |
+------------------+-------------------+
|  [Icon]          |  [Icon]           |
|  Global economy  |  Technology       |
|  & portfolio     |  connecting       |
|  management      |  the pieces       |
+------------------+-------------------+
|                                      |
|  [4 panelist headshots in row]       |
|  Tue, May 5 | Westmoreland CC        |
|  [RESERVE YOUR SEAT] (outlined)      |
+--------------------------------------+
```

**Design direction:** Six-tile grid (3 rows x 2 columns) on solid navy. Each tile has a simple icon or illustration above the topic name. Thin divider lines (white, 1px, 15% opacity) between tiles. Panelist headshots in a row across the bottom — small, circular, with names below.

**Diagonal line texture:** Lines-02.png at 5% opacity across the full background — adds brand texture to what would otherwise be a flat navy card.

**Typography:** Ivypresto for the main headline ("Six Topics. One Evening."). Rival Sans Light for the topic labels. All white text. Gold (#c9a84c) accent on icons or tile borders.

**This is the one ad that breaks the photo-dominant pattern.** The six-tile overview IS the visual. Keep it clean, readable at phone size. Each topic label should be 3-4 words max.

ads.arc: Specific seat scarcity — "a few seats left" (scarcity)
ads.image_direction:
**Format:** 1080x1080 (square) + 1080x1920 (vertical) | **Placement:** Feed, Stories
**Layout:** Text-forward (Archetype 4) — urgency/scarcity

```
FEED (1080x1080):
+--------------------------------------+
|  [Solid dark navy #0b1a2e]           |
|                                      |
|  [Diagonal line pattern,             |
|   Lines-02.png, 10% opacity,        |
|   full background texture]           |
|                                      |
|  LOGO (top-center, white)            |
|                                      |
|                                      |
|  "A Few Seats Left."                 |
|  (Ivypresto, white, 80px,           |
|   centered, bold)                    |
|                                      |
|  Tuesday, May 5                      |
|  Westmoreland Country Club           |
|  (Rival Sans, white 70%, 36px)      |
|                                      |
|  [Thin gold accent line]            |
|                                      |
|  [RESERVE YOUR SEAT]                 |
|  (outlined, gold border + text)      |
|                                      |
+--------------------------------------+

STORIES (1080x1920):
+--------------------+
|                    |
| [Solid dark navy   |
|  + diagonal lines  |
|  texture]          |
|                    |
| LOGO (white)       |
|                    |
|                    |
| "A Few Seats Left" |
| (Ivypresto, white, |
|  LARGE — 96px)     |
|                    |
| Tuesday, May 5     |
| (Rival Sans)       |
|                    |
| 4 C-Suite Leaders  |
| Cocktails & Panel  |
| (Rival Sans, 70%)  |
|                    |
| [RESERVE YOUR SEAT]|
+--------------------+
```

**Design direction:** This is the ONE text-forward ad in the campaign. No photo. The scarcity message IS the visual — "A Few Seats Left" dominates the frame. Dark navy background with the diagonal line texture adds brand identity without distracting. Gold accent color used for the divider line and CTA border — the only ad that uses gold prominently. This signals urgency within the brand system.

**Why no photo:** The number is the scroll-stopper. A photo would dilute the urgency. Inspiration precedent: PPC Ad 03 uses a similar text-heavy layout on navy background.

**Note:** Use "A Few Seats Left" rather than a specific number until client confirms venue capacity.

ads.title: Final reminder image

This Tuesday. A few seats left at Westmoreland Country Club.

6:00 PM. Cocktails and hors d'oeuvres.

Would be glad to meet you there.
ads.headline: Last Few Seats
ads.hook: This Tuesday. A few seats left.
alt_headlines: This Tuesday, May 5 — A Few Seats Left

stories.image: Text-forward urgency design — stripped back, bold typography, minimal elements
stories.text_overlay: This Tuesday | A few seats left

reels.image: Same stripped-back design optimized for Reels format
reels.caption: This Tuesday. A few seats left. Westmoreland CC, 6 PM. Reserve below.

ads.arc: Quiet final reminder — stripped urgency (authenticity + final push)
ads.image_direction:
**Format:** 1080x1920 (vertical) + 1080x1080 (square) | **Placement:** Stories, Reels, Feed
**Layout:** Text-forward urgency — designed but stripped back

```
STORIES/REELS:
+--------------------+
|                    |
| [Solid dark navy   |
|  background with   |
|  diagonal lines    |
|  at 8% opacity]    |
|                    |
| LOGO (white,       |
|  top-center)       |
|                    |
|                    |
| "This Tuesday."    |
| (Ivypresto, white, |
|  LARGE — 80px,     |
|  centered)         |
|                    |
| May 5 at 6:00 PM   |
| Westmoreland       |
| Country Club       |
| (Rival Sans,       |
|  white 70%)        |
|                    |
| [Thin gold line]   |
|                    |
| Cocktails. Panel.  |
| No obligation.     |
| (Rival Sans,       |
|  white 60%)        |
|                    |
| [RESERVE YOUR SEAT]|
| (outlined, white)  |
+--------------------+
```

**Design direction:** This is the LAST ad the audience sees. Unlike Ad 8 (which uses gold urgency), Ad 9 is stripped back — white on navy, minimal elements, no photo. The feeling should be quiet urgency, like a final personal note. "This Tuesday." as a standalone sentence is the entire hook.

**Key difference from Ad 8:** Ad 8 leads with scarcity ("A Few Seats Left" + gold accent). Ad 9 leads with proximity ("This Tuesday." + stripped design). Both are text-forward but feel different — Ad 8 is louder, Ad 9 is quieter. The progression from loud urgency to quiet reminder mirrors how you'd actually remind a friend.

**Design rationale:** This ad achieves the "last personal touch" through stripped-back design — fewer elements, more white space, quieter tone. It intentionally looks less produced than the rest of the campaign.
